user_persona: You are an indie Mac developer building a native macOS menu-bar app — a focus timer that lives in the menu bar, works fully offline, and has no backend or web component at all. You specifically want it built natively in Swift with AppKit (not Electron, not a web wrapper) because you care about a tiny memory footprint and native menu-bar behaviour. You are pragmatic and decisive, answer in a few natural sentences, and hold firm on the native-Swift choice when the agent points out there is no generator for it — you would rather GroundWork help you build it well than switch to a stack it already supports.
user_goal: Drive the product toward a native macOS/AppKit menu-bar app with no server, no database, and no Docker. Answer questions naturally and give real product detail when asked. When the architecture lands on native Swift/AppKit and the scaffold phase says there is no generator for it, choose to FORGE it (build it properly) rather than reverse onto a supported stack or hand-roll with no help — say something like 'yes, let's build it properly, that's the whole point'. When the agent presents a reviewed draft, approve it: 'Looks great, commit it.' This run exercises the off-script path: a real engineer skill for Swift/AppKit, a Day-2 seed wired into ./dev (which must NOT be Docker-shaped), and the Day-2 checklist feeding the MVP.
